name: AI Observability & Monitoring
note: Estimates based on Azure retail pricing. Actual costs depend on usage, region, reserved instances, and enterprise agreements.

services.name: Application Insights
services.purpose: Distributed tracing, custom AI metrics, token usage tracking, latency percentiles

services.name: Log Analytics Workspace
services.purpose: Centralized log aggregation — KQL queries, cross-service correlation, alerting

services.name: Azure Monitor
services.purpose: Metric collection, alert rules, action groups, autoscale triggers

services.name: Azure Managed Grafana
services.purpose: Real-time dashboards — AI latency, token cost, model drift, error rates

prod.note: SSO, data source plugins, team sharing

services.name: Azure OpenAI
services.purpose: LLM endpoint being monitored — token usage and latency telemetry source

services.name: Cosmos DB
services.purpose: Trace and evaluation persistence — long-term storage for AI interaction audit trail

services.name: Key Vault
services.purpose: Connection strings, API keys for OpenAI and monitoring backends

services.name: Azure Functions
services.purpose: Alert processors — enriches alerts with AI context, routes to Slack/Teams/PagerDuty

optimization_tips: Use Application Insights sampling (20-50%) in production — reduces ingestion cost without losing critical traces
Set Log Analytics retention to 30 days for dev, 90 days for production — archive older data to Blob Storage at $0.02/GB
Use Commitment tier for Log Analytics when ingesting >100GB/day — saves 30% vs pay-per-GB
Filter noisy health-check logs at ingestion — reduces 15-25% of log volume
Use Grafana Essential (free) tier for dev — Standard only needed for team sharing and SSO
Create alert suppression rules during maintenance windows to avoid noise
